ads.author: Kurt Stricker
ads.title: Vintage Pub Time Darts Electronic Dartboard Arcade Machine by Merit Industries - Beauty!
ads.description: Step back into the golden age of arcade fun with this authentic Pub Time Darts machine! This is the real deal – a classic 1980s Merit Industries Pub Time Darts electronic dartboard that’s been the life of the party in countless bars, game rooms, and man caves. The full-size cabinet features that iconic colorful graphics, the massive regulation-style dartboard display with all the vibrant red, blue, and gold detailing, player stations for up to 4 players (or 8 with competition mode), and the classic “throw darts” vibe complete with beer-mug branding. Condition highlights (being 100% transparent): Everything lights up beautifully – marquee, dartboard segments, buttons, score displays, and overhead lighting all glow bright and vibrant with that perfect retro arcade feel. Looks fantastic on the porch, in a game room, or tucked into a home bar setup. Game selection currently does not work (you can’t pick a game right now). This is a super common issue on these vintage Merit boards and usually comes down to a simple board-level repair, loose connection, or matrix issue that electronics hobbyists fix all the time. Parts are readily available online if you want to bring it back to full glory. Whether you’re a collector, a tinkerer who loves restoring arcade classics, or just want an awesome conversation piece that screams “Florida backyard bar,” this machine has serious character. Imagine firing up some tunes, grabbing a cold one, and turning your space into a mini pub night after night. Specs & Details: Full upright cabinet (approx. 7–8 ft tall when assembled) Coin-op ready (can be set to free play) Soft-tip darts style (safe & fun) Multiple game modes when working (301, Cricket, etc.) Heavy-duty commercial build – these things last forever

ads.title: Capcom Bowling, Upright Arcade, Capcom 1988 (Project Cab)

Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs a lot of love. - Monitor does not currently power on — likely needs new caps. - Nothing comes on at the moment; best to start troubleshooting with the power supply and work your way from there. - Sold as-is. Highlights: - Classic Capcom sports arcade with bowling gameplay. - Upright cabinet design from the golden age of arcades. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: - Price is $200, or best offer - Serious buyers only. Price Breakdown: Capcom Bowling PCS ($40) Control Panel and Trackball ($60) Cabinet ($100) CRT (dunno if its dead so $0?) Wiring, coin door, misc ($40)

ads.author: Spencer Echols
ads.title: 1979 Williams FLASH Pinball - Project / Parts Cabinet
ads.description: Selling a 1979 Williams Flash pinball machine. I’m being 100% upfront: This is a project/parts machine. I was told it was all there, but it is missing the main "guts." The Facts: MISSING: The main power transformer and the two main computer boards (MPU and Driver). INCLUDED: Cabinet, head (backbox), legs, and original backglass. CONDITION: The playfield and backglass are in decent shape for the year. The sound board and all 5 score displays are still there. STATUS: Sold AS-IS. It does not power on because of the missing parts mentioned above. This is a great donor game if you have a beat-up Flash, or a perfect project for someone who knows how to source boards and a transformer. Located in Arlington Regency Area

ads.description: If you know you know 32” Sony Trinitron CRT one of the BEST TVs ever made for retro gaming. This is the real deal not a cheap CRT. True zero input lag, buttery smooth motion, and the exact look these games were meant to have. Perfect for GameCube Smash Melee Mario Kart Double Dash N64 GoldenEye Mario Party PS2 classics OG Xbox VHS and DVD nostalgia setups These TVs are getting harder and harder to find especially in this size and condition Built like a tank incredible picture quality and that classic Trinitron color pop Includes Front AV inputs ready to plug and play Great for a retro gaming setup or basement arcade vibe Fair warning it’s heavy bring help Serious buyers only this is a collector piece

I don’t know anything about these but I did talk to an arcade dealer and he said that it’s likely inference or the sound board. This machine does have the ability to be paired with another machine. Will consider trading up or down for a slot machine, pinball machine, vintage gas pump, man cave stuff.

ads.author: Dirk Smith Johnson
ads.title: Rare Williams OXO Pinball Machine
ads.description: Very rare Williams OXO pinball machine. Previously featured at the Roanoke Pinball Museum in Virginia. This is a unique electro-mechanical game that plays tic-tac-toe! This game has been in fully working condition until recently when it started blowing a fuse during game restart. It's most likely a switch contact somewhere that's broken, but I've been too busy to diagnose it, and I need the room, so I'm willing to sell it for less than I paid to someone who wants a unique game, and is willing to tinker with it. This game is basically in original condition except for being upgraded with $200+ worth of LEDs to make it brighter and more reliable.
